instructions.name: ack_match
docs: Device marks their MatchNotification as read.
Enables garbage collection after a grace period.

accounts.name: oracle_operator
docs: Oracle operator (config.admin) receives the bond when fees don't cover compute.

accounts.name: creator
docs: Market creator receives bond refund when fees_collected >= oracle_compute_cost.

accounts.name: program_vault
docs: QD token vault — pays oracle when fees cover compute cost.

accounts.name: oracle_fee_account
docs: Oracle operator's QD token account for fee payment.

instructions.name: create_market
docs: Commit oracle-generated evidence protocol hash before market opens.
Must be called after CreateMarketPipeline completes in the Go oracle.
No positions can be placed until this PDA exists.

instructions.name: lz_receive
docs: LayerZero receive handler for incoming messages from Court.sol.
Handles FINAL_RULING message type only.

instructions.name: lz_receive_types
docs: LZ receive types handler — tells LayerZero which accounts
to include for a given incoming message.

instructions.name: notify_match
docs: Oracle writes a blinded MatchNotification for one device.
Called twice per match — once per device — with different
device and counterparty_commitment values.

instructions.name: resolve_jury
docs: Send resolution request to Court.sol via LayerZero.
Callable by anyone after deadline, if market has jury config.
Requester must hold a position >= MIN_JURY_STAKE.

instructions.name: timeout_jury
docs: Permissionless force-majeure trigger for stalled jury markets.
If no ruling arrives within JURY_TIMEOUT_SECS (14 days) of
send_resolution_request, anyone may cancel the market so that
push_payouts can return all capital.

accounts.name: caller
docs: Anyone may trigger — permissionless.

instructions.name: tip_jury
docs: Send jury compensation to Court.sol after resolution finalized.

errors.name: ForOhfour
errors.msg: if you are who you say you are then you're not who you are

errors.msg: not under-collateralised...still gains to be realised

errors.msg: Signer ETH address does not match expected artist

errors.name: InvalidMarketBinding
errors.msg: Oracle return value does not match this market's binding tag

errors.name: MaxPositionsReached
errors.msg: cant be up another one pack a new gun

errors.name: PriceManipulated
errors.msg: better you'd waited, lest you be baited

errors.name: NoPrice
errors.msg: pass in a price or call it off twice

errors.name: Tickers
errors.msg: pass in ticker no miss it or hickups

errors.name: TooSoon
errors.msg: call in too often or show stops then

errors.name: TakeProfit
errors.msg: too many ahead take profit instead

errors.name: InvalidAmount
errors.msg: Slow it up...amount is either not enough or too much.

errors.name: InvalidUser
errors.msg: Double-check who you're trying to touch.

errors.name: InvalidMint
errors.msg: We only work with stars here.

errors.name: DepositFirst
errors.msg: You must deposit before you can do this.

errors.name: InsufficientLZFee
errors.msg: Not enough lamports from resolution requester

errors.name: InvalidReturnData
errors.msg: Invalid return data from cross-program call

errors.name: NoReturnData
errors.msg: No return data from cross-program call

errors.name: PriceUncertain
errors.msg: Oracle price confidence too wide - price uncertain

errors.name: RequesterPositionTooSmall
errors.msg: Requester position too small - need 1% of market minimum

errors.name: OracleManipulated
errors.msg: Oracle price deviates too much from TWAP - possible manipulation

errors.name: TooEarlyToResolve
errors.msg: Too early to resolve - resolution time not reached

errors.name: QualifySentinelInResolution
errors.msg: outcome 254 is the qualify sentinel — cannot use as resolution

errors.name: ProtocolHashMismatch
errors.msg: oracle ran a different protocol than the one committed at market creation

errors.name: QualificationScoreTooLow
errors.msg: qualification score below minimum for this market type

errors.name: ProtocolVersionMismatch
errors.msg: protocol version mismatch — regenerate via CreateMarketPipeline

errors.name: SasNotConfigured
errors.msg: SAS credential not configured — set sas_credential in ProgramConfig

types.name: Actuary
docs: Actuary: The risk oracle that learns from market behavior.
Unlike traditional systems with preset volatility/jump parameters per asset
class, the Actuary starts conservative and discovers each ticker's true
characteristics through observation. No priors needed — the Pyth feeds
exist or they don't.
## State Size: ~104 bytes (fits comfortably in PDA)
## Confidence Model
The system uses observation count to build confidence:
- 0 obs: 0% confidence → 400 bps vol floor (conservative)
- 10 obs: 50% confidence → 200 bps vol floor
- 50 obs: 83% confidence → 68 bps vol floor
- 100 obs: 91% confidence → 36 bps vol floor
This prevents the "quiet start" attack where a ticker with no activity
gets assigned near-zero vol and offers insane leverage.

fields.name: observed_vol_bps
docs: EMA of observed absolute returns (bps). Updated on each price change.
Fast up, slow down — reacts quickly to vol spikes, decays gradually.

fields.name: max_drawdown_bps
docs: Maximum single-slot price move observed recently (bps).
Used for collar buffer calculation. Decays after calm periods.

fields.name: last_price
docs: Last oracle price (in token's native precision).
Used to calculate returns on next update.

fields.name: last_price_slot
docs: Slot number of last price update. Used for staleness penalty.

fields.name: last_trade_slot
docs: Slot number of last trade. Used for velocity decay.

fields.name: jump_count
docs: Count of recent jump events [0, 20]. Jump = move > 3σ.
Decays over time (1 per 1000 slots). Affects eta and collar.

fields.name: velocity
docs: Trade velocity score [0, 255]. Higher = more urgent activity.
Combines local (this ticker) and global (all tickers) activity.

fields.name: momentum_bps
docs: Open interest change rate [-10000, 10000] bps.
Negative = liquidation cascade risk. Affects fee multiplier.

fields.name: net_exposure
docs: Sum of (exposure × leverage) across all positions, SIGNED.
Positive = net long bias, negative = net short bias.

fields.name: total_exposure
docs: Sum of |exposure × leverage| across all positions, always positive.
Total counterparty risk regardless of direction.

fields.name: twap_price
docs: EMA smoothed price for manipulation detection.
Slower than spot price — large deviations indicate potential manipulation.

fields.name: obs_count
docs: Number of price observations. Used to compute confidence level.
Capped at 255 to fit in u8 if needed for space optimization.

fields.name: num_winners
docs: Number of expected winners. 1 = standard. >1 = contest.

fields.name: winning_splits
docs: BPS allocation per outcome. Empty = equal split.
If non-empty: len == outcomes.len(), winning entries sum to 10_000.

fields.name: beneficiaries
docs: Optional beneficiary per outcome for fee-split payouts.
Empty = no beneficiaries. If non-empty: len == outcomes.len().

fields.name: time_window_start
docs: GPS hint for the oracle — NOT a hard filter. Claude sees the raw
coordinates and reasons about proximity. GPS is spoofable so we
never gate on it; we let Claude weigh it as a soft signal.
Evidence must have timestamps overlapping this window.

fields.name: min_submissions
docs: Minimum independent device submissions for the oracle to consider
evidence sufficient. 0 = any amount is informational.

fields.name: required_tags
docs: Required tag IDs. Evidence must contain at least one above min_confidence.

fields.name: min_tag_confidence
docs: Minimum per-tag confidence in bps (5000-10000). Floor 50%.

fields.name: pipeline_routes
docs: Pipeline routes: tag→provider mappings for specialized model routing.
When evidence matches route tags, the oracle dispatches to the
specified provider URI (e.g., "switchboard:shuffler" for CoCo,
"https://api.anthropic.com" for external Claude).

fields.name: notification_domains
docs: Notification domain hashes. Oracle groups tag detections by domain
for summarization (e.g., speech.count, music.instruments).

fields.name: resolution_mode
docs: Resolution mode: 0=auto (deterministic→LLM), 1=external (Claude+Brave),
2=coco_local (TEE-only, no external APIs), 3=deterministic_only.

fields.name: max_submissions
docs: Hard cap on evidence submissions. Makes oracle processing cost bounded.
Must be 1..=MAX_EVIDENCE_SUBMISSIONS. submit_evidence rejects beyond this.

fields.name: resolution_bond
docs: Bond deposited by market creator (lamports). Covers oracle's off-chain
resolution costs (API keys, compute). Claimed by oracle after resolution.
Must be >= RESOLUTION_BOND[resolution_mode].

fields.name: jury_config
docs: Optional human jury configuration for cross-chain resolution via LayerZero.

fields.name: oracle_compute_cost
docs: Estimated oracle compute cost in QD tokens. If non-zero and fees_collected
covers it, oracle is paid from fees and SOL bond is refunded to creator.

fields.name: resolution_mode
docs: 0=auto, 1=external, 2=coco_local, 3=deterministic_only

fields.name: max_submissions
docs: Hard cap on evidence submissions. 1..=32.

fields.name: resolution_bond
docs: Resolution bond in lamports. Must be >= RESOLUTION_BOND[mode].

fields.name: jury_config
docs: Optional jury config. If Some, market supports human jury.

fields.name: oracle_compute_cost
docs: Estimated oracle compute cost in QD tokens. Set to 0 for legacy behaviour.

types.name: EvidenceSubmission
docs: One attestation per (market, submitter, nonce). Submitted by Seeker owner.
Hardware attestation data (necklace P-256 sig, BLE RSSI, motion, audio)
is encrypted on-device and stored on 0G Storage. Only a SHA256 commitment
to the ciphertext lives on-chain. At resolution, the TEE fetches the
encrypted blob from 0G, verifies the hash matches, then decrypts and
processes within the TEE boundary. Plaintext never exists outside the TEE.
Seeds: [b"evidence", market.key().as_ref(), submitter.key().as_ref(), &[nonce]]
Minimal on-chain commitment. Everything else (timestamps, GPS, audio/video
content, model pipeline results) lives in the encrypted 0G blob. The oracle
fetches the blob, verifies SHA256(blob) == attestation_hash inside CoCo,
then decrypts and processes. No self-reported metadata trusted on-chain.
Seeds: [b"evidence", market.key().as_ref(), submitter.key().as_ref(), &[nonce]]

fields.name: attestation_hash
docs: SHA256 of encrypted attestation blob stored on 0G Storage.
Oracle verifies: SHA256(downloaded_ciphertext) == attestation_hash.

fields.name: zg_root
docs: 0G Merkle root — the download address for the blob.
Stored on-chain so the oracle can fetch without a KV server.
Set by the phone at submit time from the 0G upload response.

fields.name: content_type
docs: 0 = audio, 1 = video. One byte hint so oracle knows what to expect.

fields.name: question
docs: The prediction question. Must be an objectively resolvable question
about a future real-world event.

fields.name: context
docs: Definitions, condition precedents, special terms.
e.g. "BTC price = CoinGecko daily close UTC. 'Close above' means
the final daily candle close price strictly > threshold."

fields.name: exculpatory
docs: Force majeure / exculpatory clauses.
e.g. "Market cancels if: CoinGecko is offline for >24h during
measurement period; BTC undergoes a chain halt >6h; exchange
delisting removes >50% of CoinGecko's price sources."

fields.name: resolution_source
docs: Optional hint for the oracle on where to find resolution data.
e.g. "check CoinGecko", "check AP News", "check NBA.com"

fields.name: outcomes
docs: The possible outcomes. Fixed at creation. Minimum 2.

fields.name: num_outcomes
docs: Cached outcome count — MUST stay in sync with outcomes.len().
Used by etc.rs::update_price_accumulator TWAP loop.

fields.name: sb_feed
docs: Single Switchboard Pull Feed for this market.
Oracle writes resolution result here.

fields.name: winning_sides
docs: All winning outcome indices. For single-winner markets: vec![winning_outcome].
For multi-winner contests: multiple indices (up to num_winners).

fields.name: winning_splits
docs: BPS allocation per outcome index. Set at creation.
Empty = equal split among winners. If non-empty: len == num_outcomes,
only entries at winning_sides indices matter, must sum to 10_000.

fields.name: num_winners
docs: Expected number of winners. 1 = standard prediction market.
>1 = contest/tournament with multiple winning outcomes.

fields.name: beneficiaries
docs: Optional beneficiary addresses parallel to outcomes.
Used for fee-split payouts to side sponsors/creators.
Empty = no beneficiaries. If non-empty: len == num_outcomes.

fields.name: challenge_count
docs: Number of times this resolution has been challenged and re-evaluated.

fields.name: challenged
docs: Whether a challenge is currently pending (oracle re-run in progress).

fields.name: winner_weight_per_outcome
docs: Per-outcome winner weight — populated during calculate_weights.
Parallel to outcomes. Only indices in winning_sides are meaningful.
Used by push_payouts for split-based pot partitioning.

types.name: MarketEvidence
docs: Per-market evidence requirements. Created alongside market when creator
opts in to the evidence layer. Separate PDA so existing markets are
unaffected — Market struct layout does not change.
Seeds: [b"market_evidence", market.key().as_ref()]

fields.name: oracle_claimed
docs: True after oracle operator has claimed the resolution bond.

types.name: MatchNotification
docs: On-chain notification of a match, written by the matching oracle.
One PDA per matched device. Counterparty is blinded.
PDA seeds: [b"match_notif", device.key().as_ref(), &slot.to_le_bytes()]

fields.name: counterparty_commitment
docs: Nonce derivation (oracle-side):
nonce = keccak256(device_A || device_B || category_hash || slot)
This makes the nonce unique per match event, non-reusable across
categories or slots, and non-guessable by either party independently.
The oracle sends the same nonce to both matched devices off-chain.
Neither device can discover the counterparty without receiving the nonce.

fields.name: oracle_attestation
docs: CoCo oracle signature over:
SHA256(device || counterparty_commitment || category_hash
|| similarity_bps || slot)

fields.name: similarity_bps
docs: Bilateral harmonic mean similarity, 0-10000 bps.

types.name: PipelineRoute
docs: A pipeline route maps a set of tags to a processing endpoint.
The oracle uses this to dispatch evidence to specialized processors
(e.g., Whisper for transcription, embedder for semantic matching).
The shuffler is NOT routed via model_class — it's fixed TEE infrastructure
triggered by provider_uri ("switchboard:shuffler"). model_class here
tells the oracle which model class produces input for this route.

fields.name: tags
docs: Tag IDs that trigger this route (keccak hashes).

fields.name: provider_uri
docs: Provider URI. Registered models: "switchboard:<model_id>".
Fixed infra: "switchboard:shuffler". External: "https://api.anthropic.com".

fields.name: model_class
docs: Pipeline role of the model that feeds this route.
0=CLASSIFIER, 1=TRANSCRIBER, 2=EMBEDDER. Matches 0G model classification.

fields.name: priority
docs: Priority (lower = higher priority). Routes evaluated in order.

fields.name: is_direct_evidence
docs: If true, this route's output counts as direct evidence for resolution
(vs. supplementary context for the LLM).

fields.name: verdict_hint
docs: Optional hint for the oracle on what this route's verdict means.

types.name: Position
docs: Position PDA — one per (market, user, outcome).
Lifecycle: created in place_order, weight computed in calculate_weights,
then atomically paid out + closed (data zeroed, rent → creator) in
push_payouts. Zeroed discriminator prevents double-processing.

fields.name: revealed_confidence
docs: 0 = unrevealed. Set during reveal phase of calculate_weights.

fields.name: accuracy_percentile
docs: Raw accuracy score for bucket lookup. Actual percentile
is computed in the weigh phase from AccuracyBuckets.

fields.name: orchestrator
docs: Switchboard Function pubkey of the trusted orchestrator.
Only feeds signed by this function are accepted for resolution.

fields.name: slot
docs: Slot at which the match was produced. Used in PDA seed for uniqueness.
Oracle passes current slot; on-chain record stores it for ack/close.

fields.name: collar_dollar_seconds
docs: Time-integrated economic capital: sum of (collar_dollars × seconds).
RAROC denominator — how much capital was at risk, for how long.

fields.name: attestation_hash
docs: SHA256 of encrypted attestation blob uploaded to 0G Storage.
All metadata (timestamps, GPS, tags, model pipeline results) lives
in the blob. TEE verifies this hash before decrypting at resolution.

fields.name: zg_root
docs: 0G Merkle root — the download address on 0G Storage.
Stored on-chain so the oracle can locate the blob without a KV server.

fields.name: content_type
docs: 0=audio (necklace + Seeker), 1=video (Seeker only).
One byte hint so oracle knows what decoder to apply.

fields.name: nonce
docs: Allows multiple submissions per device per market (different sessions).
